titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Circumambulates or Performs Any of the Rites Without Ablution
Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Circumambulates or Performs Any of the Rites Without Ablution

segments: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تَقْضِيَ اَلْمَنَاسِكَ كُلَّهَا عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ إِلاَّ اَلطَّوَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ اَلْوُضُوءُ أَفْضَلُ ».
arabic.text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تَقْضِيَ اَلْمَنَاسِكَ كُلَّهَا عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ إِلاَّ اَلطَّوَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ اَلْوُضُوءُ أَفْضَلُ ».

segments:
Hadith.2810 - It is narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"There is no harm in completing all the rituals without being in a state of wudu, except for the tawaf around the Kaaba. However, performing wudu is better."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2810 - It is narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"There is no harm in completing all the rituals without being in a state of wudu, except for the tawaf around the Kaaba. However, performing wudu is better."

narratorChain.raw: رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Circumambulates or Performs Any of the Rites Without Ablution

segments: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَافَ اَلْفَرِيضَةَ وَ هُوَ عَلَى غَيْرِ طُهْرٍ قَالَ «يَتَوَضَّأُ وَ يُعِيدُ طَوَافَهُ فَإِنْ كَانَ تَطَوُّعاً تَوَضَّأَ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ.
arabic.text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَافَ اَلْفَرِيضَةَ وَ هُوَ عَلَى غَيْرِ طُهْرٍ قَالَ «يَتَوَضَّأُ وَ يُعِيدُ طَوَافَهُ فَإِنْ كَانَ تَطَوُّعاً تَوَضَّأَ وَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ.

segments:
Hadith.2811 - It is narrated from al-Ala' through Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams (as)  who said: I asked him about a man who performed the obligatory tawaf without being in a state of purity (wudu).
Imam (as) said: "He should perform wudu and repeat his tawaf. If it was a voluntary tawaf, he should perform wudu and pray two rak‘ahs."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2811 - It is narrated from al-Ala' through Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams (as)  who said: I asked him about a man who performed the obligatory tawaf without being in a state of purity (wudu).
Imam (as) said: "He should perform wudu and repeat his tawaf. If it was a voluntary tawaf, he should perform wudu and pray two rak‘ahs."

narratorChain.raw: 2811 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Circumambulates or Performs Any of the Rites Without Ablution

segments: رِوَايَةِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَطُوفَ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلنَّافِلَةَ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ ثُمَّ يَتَوَضَّأَ وَ يُصَلِّيَ وَ إِنْ طَافَ مُتَعَمِّداً عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ وَ لْيُصَلِّ ».
arabic.text: رِوَايَةِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَطُوفَ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلنَّافِلَةَ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ ثُمَّ يَتَوَضَّأَ وَ يُصَلِّيَ وَ إِنْ طَافَ مُتَعَمِّداً عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ وَ لْيُصَلِّ ».

segments:
Hadith.2812 - In a narration from Ubayd ibn Zurarah, he reported from Imam (as) that he said:
"There is no harm if a man performs the voluntary tawaf without being in a state of wudu, then performs wudu and prays. However, if he intentionally performs tawaf without wudu, he should perform wudu and then pray."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2812 - In a narration from Ubayd ibn Zurarah, he reported from Imam (as) that he said:
"There is no harm if a man performs the voluntary tawaf without being in a state of wudu, then performs wudu and prays. However, if he intentionally performs tawaf without wudu, he should perform wudu and then pray."

narratorChain.raw: وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ :

parts.text: َ فِي رِوَايَةِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ :

titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Circumambulates or Performs Any of the Rites Without Ablution

segments: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ سَعَى بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ فَسَعَى ثَلاَثَةَ أَشْوَاطٍ أَوْ أَرْبَعَةً ثُمَّ بَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَمَّ سَعْيَهُ بِغَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ وَ لَوْ أَتَمَّ مَنَاسِكَهُ بِوُضُوءٍ كَانَ أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ ».
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ سَعَى بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ فَسَعَى ثَلاَثَةَ أَشْوَاطٍ أَوْ أَرْبَعَةً ثُمَّ بَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَمَّ سَعْيَهُ بِغَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ وَ لَوْ أَتَمَّ مَنَاسِكَهُ بِوُضُوءٍ كَانَ أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ ».

segments:
Hadith.2813 - It is narrated by Safwan from Yahya al-Azraq, who said: I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who performed the sa‘i between Safa and Marwa, completing three or four circuits, then urinated and completed the rest of his sa‘i without wudu.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, but if he had completed his rituals with wudu, it would have been more preferable to me."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2813 - It is narrated by Safwan from Yahya al-Azraq, who said: I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who performed the sa‘i between Safa and Marwa, completing three or four circuits, then urinated and completed the rest of his sa‘i without wudu.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, but if he had completed his rituals with wudu, it would have been more preferable to me."

narratorChain.raw: 2813 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ يَحْيَى اَلْأَزْرَقِ قَالَ
